title: The Trojan Universality Class and Nonlinear Dynamics
author: Doucette, Doug
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This book introduces the Trojan Universality Class (TUC), a unifying framework explaining why diverse systems—from asteroids to lasers, molecules to heartbeats—exhibit unexpectedly long-lived coherence. It argues that persistent stability is not an accident but a structural consequence of a specific dynamical architecture: two dominant oscillatory modes, nonlinearly coupled yet separated by a persistent spectral gap. This gap suppresses low-order resonances and throttles energy transport, forcing the system into a near-integrable state where motion is confined by invariant geometric structures. The book rigorously defines the TUC through local symplectic equivalence and normal-form theory, demonstrating its application across astrophysics, chemistry, engineering, and physiology. It provides a practical protocol for identifying Trojan systems and shows how knowledge of their stability, failure modes (such as resonance overlap and gap collapse), and exponential timescales can be lawfully transferred between radically different domains. Ultimately, the TUC transforms the study of persistence from a collection of special cases into a coherent method for prediction, diagnosis, and design.
